title: Anthropic and Meta open talks on a $10 billion compute lease
subtitle: Anthropic would rent GPUs sitting inside Meta's data centers for two years, turning Zuckerberg's AI buildout into a cloud business overnight.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: CNBC, the New York Times and CNN reported on July 17, 2026 that Anthropic is in early talks with Meta to lease AI compute capacity in a deal one source pegged at around $10 billion over two years. Anthropic would pay Meta in monthly installments, and either side could exit early. The talks come weeks after Anthropic signed a similar arrangement with xAI's parent SpaceX for capacity at the Colossus 1 data center.

The story matters because it drags Meta into the cloud business. Zuckerberg told shareholders in May that entering cloud computing was 'definitely on the table' — companies were approaching Meta 'almost every week' for either its models or its spare GPUs. A signed Anthropic deal would put Meta directly alongside AWS, Azure and Google Cloud in the market for frontier-lab compute, using capacity Meta built for its own training runs.

Anthropic's compute mix is starting to look unusual. It already ships on AWS Bedrock and Google Cloud. It has a Samsung 2nm custom chip conversation running. It has hired Meta's Tom Blomfield onto its compute team. And now it may rent from Meta and xAI. Frontier labs used to be tied to a single hyperscaler — OpenAI to Microsoft, Anthropic to AWS and Google. That model is breaking. The IPO pressure Anthropic is under before a rumored 2027 listing is turning compute procurement into a multi-vendor sport.

For learners: watch which company controls the GPUs, not just which company writes the model weights. In the current market, the number that decides whether a lab can train its next model is a lease agreement, not a research paper. Understanding the compute supply chain — who owns the fabs, who builds the data centers, who leases capacity to whom — is now core AI literacy, not a niche finance beat.

sources.title: Anthropic in early talks with Meta to acquire compute power — CNBC
Meta in talks to rent some of its billions in AI infrastructure to Anthropic — CNN Business
Anthropic in talks to lease Meta computing power in $10 billion deal — Yahoo Finance
